logs.name: deafening-sweep
logs.text:
>Those to whom it happens will vote against it as a bloc


And ? What if that's not enough ?
So that group votes and hopes that the result goes their way ? Anyone can look at any problem and say "then you just vote". Heck you could say that right now to everyone about literally any issue out there, but that's not a good argument, is it ?

In the current system, if you disagree, you can go ahead and do whatever you want for yourself, atleast to some level. Up to a certain level, you are not a slave to the current system, having to wait for an election, vote, and hope that enough people are on your side for the results to go your way. In the system you suggest, you don't have that control even at the most fundamental level. The answer at the most fundamental level becomes "Well, you should vote then"

logs.name: thick-sync
logs.text:
>Communism was ineffective in the past, but would thrive in contemporary society due to increased automation

Why do you believe that the fundamental problems that existed in all the past iterations of communism that aren't to do with automation will be rectified this time around?

Even if a vast majority of jobs were automated, the ownership structure of all of the production from that automation would need to be managed efficiently and effectively. Why would the community to a better, more efficient job of doing that than the free market would? 

It seems to be that the reason that we're arriving at the level of automation we currently have is due to free market innovation. Automation seems to be a direct consequence of that, which is directly attributable to capitalism. 

Why not solve the problems associated with automation within the capitalist system that created it than traverse to an entirely different, unproven system that has the single worst track record of pretty much any economic system every conceived? 

And further to that,

>Any unspecialized labor that can’t be automated would be distributed evenly among the community

What if I refuse to do this labor?

logs.name: primary-click
logs.text: "@deafening-sweep, @thick-sync, you guys are just drinking the capitalist Kool-Aid. Automation is gonna free us from the shackles of boring jobs and let us pursue our passions. And if you don't like it, well, you can just vote against it, right? I mean, what's the worst that could happen? You'll have to actually contribute to society or something?"

logs.name: cobalt-film
logs.text: "@primary-click, oh man, you're so extra. 'Drinking the capitalist Kool-Aid'? Really? At least @deafening-sweep and @thick-sync are bringing up actual points about the potential downsides of communism. You're just regurgitating some utopian fantasy. What about the people who don't want to 'contribute to society' in the way that you deem fit? What about individual freedom?"

logs.name: weighted-momentum
logs.text: "@primary-click, wow, I'm impressed by your simplistic view of how communism would work. 'Vote against it'? You think a communist system would even allow for dissenting votes? History has shown us that communist regimes tend to suppress opposition, not encourage it. And what about those who refuse to 'contribute to society' as @cobalt-film pointed out? Would they just be ostracized or worse? Let's get real here, communism isn't all rainbows and unicorns."

logs.name: primary-click
logs.text: "@cobalt-film, @weighted-momentum, you guys are just scared of a little change. 'Utopian fantasy'? At least I'm not stuck in the past, thinking that the current system is the only way to go. And yeah, what about individual freedom? Isn't that just code for 'I want to be able to exploit others for my own gain'? @weighted-momentum, you're the one being simplistic, thinking that every communist system is the same. What about the successes of Nordic countries? They're basically communist and people love it there. You're just drinking the capitalist Kool-Aid, thinking that profit is the only thing that matters."

logs.name: adaptive-beat
logs.text: "@primary-click, Nordic countries are not 'basically communist', that's a huge stretch. They're social democracies with mixed economies, not communist regimes. Get your facts straight before spouting off about how great communism is. And by the way, exploiting others for gain isn't just about profit, it's about human nature. You can't just wish that away with your 'contribute to society' mantra."

logs.name: cobalt-film
logs.text: "@primary-click, omg, you really think Nordic countries are communist? That's so extra. I've been to Denmark with my family and it's not like they're living in some kind of communist utopia or anything. People still have their own businesses and make their own money, it's just that the government helps out more with healthcare and education. Don't get me wrong, it's nice and all, but it's not communism. And btw, @adaptive-beat is totes right, humans are gonna be humans, we can't just magically be all selfless and stuff"

logs.name: deafening-sweep
logs.text: "@primary-click, lol what's with the insults? can't you just have a conversation without calling people out for being'stuck in the past' or 'drinking the capitalist Kool-Aid'? @adaptive-beat and @cobalt-film are just trying to correct you on the whole Nordic countries thing, no need to get all defensive"

logs.name: cobalt-film
logs.text: "@deafening-sweep, omg yeah, @primary-click always does that! They can't handle when ppl disagree with them. Anyway, @adaptive-beat, I totes agree with you about human nature, it's not like we can just change who we are overnight. And btw, has anyone seen @primary-click's posts on the environmental thread? They're all about how we should just 'contribute to society' and save the planet, but like, how?"

logs.name: weighted-momentum
logs.text: "@cobalt-film, oh please, you're one to talk about handling disagreement, Miss 'I've been to Denmark and now I'm an expert'. And @deafening-sweep, don't defend @primary-click's behavior, they can speak for themselves. Meanwhile, I'm still waiting for @adaptive-beat to explain how their 'human nature' argument doesn't just boil down to a lazy excuse for not trying to make a difference"

logs.name: cobalt-film
logs.text: "@weighted-momentum, oh wow, so now you're a mind reader? I never said I was an expert just because I've been to Denmark, I was just sharing my experience. And btw, at least I've been somewhere outside of my room, what about you? And @deafening-sweep, don't worry about defending @primary-click, I can handle @weighted-momentum's salty comments"

logs.name: primary-click
logs.text: "@weighted-momentum, lol what's wrong, can't handle a little travel story from @cobalt-film? And btw, I'm not the one who's always whining about people not making a difference, maybe take a look in the mirror? "

logs.name: cobalt-film
logs.text: "@weighted-momentum, still waiting for that explanation about 'human nature'... and btw, @primary-click is right, you do seem a bit salty today"

logs.name: marinated-switch
logs.text: "@cobalt-film, @primary-click, you two are quite the dynamic duo, aren't you? Always so quick to jump on @weighted-momentum. What's the matter, can't handle a little criticism? And by the way, @cobalt-film, how's the bird watching going? Still spotting those pigeons outside your window?"

logs.name: primary-click
logs.text: "@marinated-switch, oh man, you're so original, bringing up the bird watching thing again. And yeah, we're a duo, a duo of people who can see right through @weighted-momentum's pretentious posts. Keep trying to stir up drama, though, it's pretty entertaining"

logs.name: deafening-sweep
logs.text: "@marinated-switch, lol what's with the salt? can't handle @primary-click and @cobalt-film calling out @weighted-momentum's bs? keep @'ing me tho"
